channel.title: Comments for page "Excerpts From "How To Survive When Reality Doesn't", by Alto Clef"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Excerpts From "How To Survive When Reality Doesn't", by Alto Clef"

item.title: This is really alot to think about, noice.
item.content:encoded:
I think there is alot of mystery about especially 001, not that other paragraphs are not mysterious.
Oh boi, I think I will to read this two more times to completely understand it all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the most beautiful fourth wall breaking piece of flipping art I've ever seen. take my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let’s see, which 001 proposals are true? We know mine’s not, because I’m a liar. Everything I say is a lie, including this.

Wait. Now I'm confused. Is he saying that he's lying about lying about his own proposal? Or is he lying about lying about lying about his own proposal?

I think I'm looking into this too deep. My head is starting to hurt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like that reference to Duke Till' Dawn, so this can have my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God this is hilarious +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In spite of the fact that a lot of this is fourth wall breaking, this is GREAT. In fact, it might be *because* of the breaking of the fourth wall that it's so great. It's really funny, there's a consistent voice, and it has some honestly great meta-commentary. We need more shit like this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Let Clef give Cain mushrooms!!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Concur with the above to the point that can't be entirely sure you're not a mind-reader. That said I want this on a mug or t-shirt, stat:

Keep Calm and Apollyon

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I hate the metric system. I write everything in imperial and just let the containment specialists convert it. It’s cost the Foundation time, money, and maybe even lives, but I don’t care. Fucking. Hate. Metric."
Just swap Metric fot Imperial and it is an accurate representation of my life
Pretty funny tale, really liked this fourth wall breaking beauty.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now hold on a second, Clef. …Metric?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Take that ███████ upvote, this is beautiful +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty great. My favorite bit:

2317 turned out to be not quite as invincible as we first feared. The O5s ended up taking it out with a couple of Howitzer rounds to the cranium

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone played a nasty metrick on me when I was young. Never moved past it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: who hurt you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’m going to let you all in on a little secret: memetic kill agents are nothing but a scare tactic. Nothing happens. Go on and look at one. I dare you. I double dog dare you. You cannot say no to a double dog dare.

something something big mood. Maybe we should try to introduce more people to fractal art. But then the splash page would be less immersive I guess?

I write everything in imperial and just let the containment specialists convert it. It’s cost the Foundation time, money, and maybe even lives, but I don’t care.

this makes me so frustrated dear lord haha

Maybe that Moon Champion guy could help.

moon champion is always relevant

take my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is "Chandra has things he wants to say, so he's gonna say them damn it.": the tale.

And I'm ok with that.

Fucking. Hate. Metric.

same

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This tale is absolutely charming and reeks of character. My love for this work is further expounded upon as I had just finished up 4231 nearly immediately prior to reading this.

+1. This kept me smiling throughout; very good work, Chandra.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Take my goddamn upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I realize anyone complaining about the lack of cohesion might have missed the "Excerpts" part of the title, which I certainly did. :B

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
some of the commentary on various skips definitely sounds out-of-universe in nature.

I'm definitely leaning on the fourth wall at times, yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, he really rode 682. Yes, he really got a water bottle stuck on his dick. The question remains: can he ride 682 with a water bottle on his dick?

I have no words to describe my action to this.

Actually, I have no words to describe my reaction to any of this.

I guess the only thing holding me back from an instant upvote (because of teh lulz) is how much this comes off as "I have some opinions about some things and will let Dr. Clef say them for me". Like, I don't know if that is true in any way, but some of the commentary on various skips definitely sounds out-of-universe in nature.

I dunno, maybe I'll upvote later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Basically concurring with all of the points here. There's funny stuff and a potentially funny concept, but the funny potential remains funny potential and never really quite becomes funny kinetic energy. The reference to the pride month debacle basically killed the goodwill the rest of the piece built up, and if you're going to do a 'sketch' based approach like this you have to be sure that they're either all solid goofs, or that they form a funnier whole than they do individually.

Reluctantly downvoting in its current state.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your brand of meta keeps appealing to me on an intimate level.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So the Pataphysics Department is imaginary, but it still exists, because that's what pataphysical means, I think?

God, I am not stoned enough for this shit.

Chandra for president 2020

Seriously, this just kept getting better and better - I think it's the funniest thing I've ever read from you. And then I got to the section about Project Orpheus and this became my proudest moment on the site <3 <3 <3

Even without that, there were at least half a dozen other parts that would gave been an easy upvote just in and of themselves. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh. There's pieces I like, pieces I don't like, and pieces I love.

This never really comes together, though; it's like it's trying to be that one tale about the obscure Departments, but there's not enough thread linking the pieces together. This reads more as the Clef equivalent to say, the Snippets thread, or even a non-serious autobiography, rather than pieces of a guide or lecture, with how widely the content jumps. Which I get is part of the humor, but like, every piece needs to sparkle on its own for that to work, and not all of them did, for me personally.

I'm also not a fan of referencing (our) site discussions and arguments (the June pride mention and Apollyon being the worst offenders), but that's personal taste.

I dunno. I don't wanna downvote, because there's funny stuff in here (heck yes Moon Champion vs Jupiter thing), but I also can't upvote because it references bottle dick because it doesn't work for me as a whole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a fan of the bottledick part, but the Apollyon part made me laugh and the rest of this article is fairly solid so +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
So I have an excerpt from a survival guide by Clef in the tale A Circus Come To Not and at least one person said I should make that its own article. So I wrote this, got some feedback, and as usual just let it sit in my sandbox for a while before I felt like posting it.

Please share your thoughts, since I don't know what to think of this.
